refactorIssues.status: public-disabled; production residue removed by VER-138

legacyPolicy.resolution: production legacy lifecycle residue removed by VER-138; only explicit scheduler-safety paths remain

classifications.core: Agent-owned scheduler, workspace, runner, evidence, lifecycle-tool, validation, and traceability proof.
classifications.extension: Review, CI, merge, dashboard, registry, model-routing, tracker-adapter, and raw GraphQL surfaces; useful but not core proof.
classifications.legacy: Test-only compatibility fixture that is not public config and does not count toward source-faithful proof.
classifications.live-e2e: Credential-gated certification that remains separate from local fake-gated core proof.

scenarios.summary: AgentOS preserves issue-first, no-PR already-satisfied completion without requiring pull request metadata.

evidence.testName: records already-satisfied no-op handoffs without requiring a PR

proofCommands: npm test -- tests/orchestrator.test.ts -t "records already-satisfied no-op" --reporter verbose

scenarios.summary: One-PR implementation remains supported while agent-owned lifecycle evidence and strict workflow defaults govern completion.

evidence.testName: runs automated reviewers before moving an implemented PR to Human Review
keeps root and base template workflows as strict agent-owned defaults

scenarios.summary: Multi-PR handoffs preserve PR arrays and roles instead of collapsing issue state to one URL.

evidence.testName: captures multi-PR handoff parsing behavior
persists multiple PR outputs without collapsing issue state to one PR
parses explicit PR roles and selects review and merge targets

proofCommands: npm test -- tests/characterization.test.ts tests/issue-state.test.ts tests/orchestrator.test.ts --reporter verbose

scenarios.summary: Successful runner output without required agent-owned tracker evidence becomes local human-required state and is not repaired by scheduler-authored lifecycle progress.

evidence.testName: marks missing agent-owned evidence human-required without duplicating lifecycle writes and remains stable after restart

scenarios.summary: Partial evidence remains stable after restart across start-only, start-plus-PR, start-plus-handoff, and complete evidence states.

evidence.testName: keeps start-only partial evidence human-required and stable after restart
keeps start-and-pr partial evidence human-required and stable after restart
keeps start-and-handoff partial evidence human-required when validation evidence is missing
records passed agent-owned evidence before completing the run

scenarios.summary: All allowed no-agent-can-act scheduler safety reasons are enumerated, logged as scheduler safety, and kept bookkeeping-only.

evidence.testName: allows enumerated scheduler safety writes in agent-owned mode and logs the safety reason
rejects scheduler safety writes without an allowed reason or with substantive content

scenarios.summary: Review, CI, and merge remain extension surfaces; architecture checks prove core scheduler writes route through lifecycle boundaries and the controller stays thin.

evidence.testName: reports direct tracker writes and raw tracker mutations in core scheduler code
reports lifecycle controller imports of extension implementations

scenarios.summary: Raw Linear GraphQL remains unavailable unless separately opted in through client tracker tools.

evidence.testName: advertises only for agent-owned Linear workflows with auth
returns structured availability and unsupported-tool failures
strictly gates production agent-owned lifecycle mode
keeps root and base template workflows as strict agent-owned defaults

scenarios.summary: Generated harnesses include app-legibility proof hooks and doctor checks enforce the proof contract.

evidence.testName: installs a profile without overwriting existing files by default
records configured proof commands without persisting secret-bearing command strings
